stories.headline: Iran Reportedly Seized Tanker After Clash With US Near Hormuz
stories.summary: The US and Iran clashed near the Strait of Hormuz, an escalation that threatens to further fracture a fragile ceasefire as the two sides discuss a permanent end to the war.

stories.headline: US, Iran clash in Hormuz as war escalates: What happened, why it matters
stories.summary: A ceasefire between the US and Iran has been in place since April 8. Both sides are yet to agree on a peace deal.

stories.headline: Russia, Ukraine trade fire, blame despite Victory Day ceasefire

stories.summary: Iran accuses US of breaching ceasefire near Hormuz as CENTCOM says forces responded to Iranian attacks.

stories.headline: Trump says US-Iran ceasefire still in place after exchange of fire in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Iran accuses the US of violating the truce, alleging it targeted an oil tanker and carried out attacks on coastal areas.

stories.headline: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of breaching Victory Day ceasefire

stories.headline: Food Prices Rise to Highest in Three Years on Iran War Costs
stories.summary: Global food prices climbed to their highest level in more than three years as the Iran war disrupted supply chains, raising the prospect of larger bills for shoppers.

stories.headline: Amid Ukraine’s daring assaults, Russia scales back Victory Day celebrations
stories.summary: An important date in the Russian calendar, May 9 will be a slimmed-down affair with less military showcasing.

stories.summary: A Malta-flagged tanker, carrying one million barrels of crude oil, has successfully reached the western coast of South Korea after passing through the Strait of Hormuz in mid-April.

stories.summary: US stocks and gold are hurtling toward a fourth year of double-digit gains, rallies so exceptional that there have been few such episodes in history, according to Bank of America Corp. strategists.

stories.summary: Nigel Farage's populist party Reform UK has made early gains in local elections with wins in Labour heartlands.

stories.headline: Shares of India’s Top Lender Slump on Interest-Income Miss
stories.summary: State Bank of India’s shares dropped sharply, posting their biggest decline in nearly two years, after the nation’s top lender by assets reported weaker-than-expected net interest income.

stories.headline: Trump’s Latest 10% Global Tariffs Ruled Unlawful by US Trade Court
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump’s 10% global tariffs were declared unlawful by a federal trade court just months after the US Supreme Court vacated earlier levies he’d imposed. Brendan Murray has the details on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Clariant CEO: Demand Remains Solid Despite Hormuz Blockade
stories.summary: Demand for chemical products remains high, even though the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has complicated supply chains, says Conrad Keijzer, CEO of Swiss chemicals company Clariant. He spoke on "The Pulse with Francine Lacqua" on Bloomberg TV.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: BofA Didn’t Have Block Trade Mandate at Time of Segantii Calls
stories.summary: Bank of America Corp. didn’t have a mandate to work on a block trade in Esprit Holdings Ltd. when it had phone calls with Segantii Capital Management to discuss the deal, a prosecution witness said during the hedge fund’s trial for insider trading.

stories.headline: Oil’s Price Surge Spurs Nigeria’s Flip From Discount to Darling

stories.headline: DBS Sells Private Credit Fund Yielding Up to 20% to Rich Clients
stories.summary: Singapore-based firm Granite Asia has engaged the private banking arm of DBS Group Holding Ltd. to raise additional capital for its first private credit vehicle, people familiar with the matter said.

stories.headline: Novo Nordisk’s Ozempic India Price Cut Boosts Obesity Drug Sales
stories.summary: Novo Nordisk A/S saw a 40% surge in sales of its diabetes and weight-loss drugs in India last month, after steep price cuts boosted demand for its branded treatments even as generic GLP-1 therapies flood the market.

stories.headline: Trump gives EU ultimatum deadline to approve trade deal with US
stories.summary: Trump's deadline to the European Union came as a trade court ruled his global tariff policy violated US law.

stories.headline: US Stocks Rise as Tech Outlook Offsets War Worries: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A rally in technology stocks is lifting US index futures as investors wait for the monthly jobs report. Oil fluctuated while the Trump administration pushed for a peace deal with Iran.

stories.headline: Trump pledged to free Jimmy Lai. Now U.S. lawmakers want action at China summit.
stories.summary: The president previously committed to helping the 78-year-old Lai, a symbol of Hong Kong’s pro-democracy movement who has been imprisoned since 2020.

stories.headline: Rwandan singer dies as he was being released from prison

stories.headline: 'I wouldn't pay it' - Trump on USA ticket price

stories.summary: Federico Valverde went to hospital after a dressing room row with Real Madrid team-mate Aurelien Tchouameni which left him with a head injury after he "accidentally hit a table".

stories.headline: Global Central Banks Boost Their Holdings of Malaysia Bonds

stories.summary: The mayor of Los Angeles is running for a second term as a former ally and a reality TV star tap into widespread voter angst.

stories.headline: Solomon Islands to get new leader after PM ousted in no-confidence vote
